query: I'm starting a new project and want to use SDD. can you walk me through the methodology from scratch? I've never used speckit before but I installed the CLI already
which checklist domains should I pick for SPEC-012? it's a REST API that handles webhook delivery with retry logic and signature verification. I'm thinking maybe api, resilience, and security?
the simplicity gate is failing on my spec for the notification service. it says the acceptance criteria count exceeds the threshold. how do I reduce complexity without cutting important requirements?
can you explain the consensus protocol to me? I see it mentioned in the autopilot docs but I don't understand when the 3 agents vote and what happens if they disagree on a gate result

I need to decompose our billing system feature into multiple specs for a technical roadmap. it covers subscription management, invoice generation, payment processing, and usage metering. what's the right way to break this up?
what's the difference between specify plan and specify tasks? I ran plan and got a plan.md but now I'm not sure if I should run tasks next or if there's something else in between
how do I write testable acceptance criteria for my spec? I keep getting vague requirements like 'the system should be fast' and the gate validator is rejecting them
I installed the tdd-mandate preset but I'm not sure how it changes the workflow. does it add extra gates? do I need to write tests before implementation now?
my team lead wants me to create a technical roadmap for our Q3 roadmap. we have 8 features that need specs. how do I set up the technical roadmap template and track dependencies between specs?

I just ran specify checklist and got a bunch of domains I don't recognize — like 'observability' and 'data-integrity'. can you explain what each domain covers and help me pick the right ones for a CRUD API?
where does grill me fit into the SDD workflow? does it replace /speckit.clarify or is it something different?
interview me about this raw client brief and walk every branch of the design tree before I commit to a spec. ask one question at a time with your recommended answer first
run autopilot on the SPEC-027 workflow. the file is at docs/ai/specs/027-calendar-sync/SPEC-027-workflow.md. I already filled in all the phase prompts and the constitution is set up
execute the full speckit workflow autonomously for SPEC-013. workflow file is populated at docs/ai/specs/SPEC-013-workflow.md. go through all 7 phases with auto-commits
can you add a new MCP tool for listing OmniFocus perspectives? I need it to return the name, ID, and icon for each built-in and custom perspective
fix the TypeScript build error in src/tools/primitives/tasks.ts. the Zod schema is failing because I'm using z.union instead of z.discriminatedUnion
review PR #42 on the omnifocus-mcp repo and leave inline comments on any issues you find. focus on the OmniJS script correctness
run pnpm test and tell me which test suites are failing. I think the review-tools contracts have a regression
set up biome linting for the project. I need to configure the rules for TypeScript strict mode and add a pre-commit hook
can you resolve the review threads on PR #38? the reviewer approved but the threads are still open and I can't merge
what's the current git status? I want to see if there are any uncommitted changes before I start the next feature
kick off the autonomous pipeline for SPEC-019. the workflow is at specs/SPEC-019-workflow.md. run all phases as subagents with gate validation and consensus
